text:
There once was a very ordinary garden with three apples trees. One apple lay ripe and red, another was green, and the last one a sprightly golden hue. Every day, a lazy, middle-aged man named Peter passed by this garden on his way to work.

One particular day, with a heavy rain forecasted, Peter paused, wondering if the adventure he'd envisioned—growing a spritely apple tree and sharing its fruits with strangers—might miraculously come to fruition. He didn't know what magical forces would inspire such a whimsical dream, but something within him felt compelled to try.

Night fell, and under the light of the street lamp, the peculiar orchard sparkled. Peter’s determination took root in the night. By morning, the golden apple had sprouted. It had a shimmer to it, as if the sunshine itself glowed with warmth.

Excited, Peter brought the sprout to his home garden, nurtured it tenderly, and watched it grow
